# EdiVal-Agent: Object-Centric Framework for Fine-Grained Evaluation of Multi-Turn Editing

Extracted from [arXiv:2509.13399](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.13399)

## Core Concept

This paper introduces techniques for object-centric framework for fine-grained evaluation of multi-turn editing. The work addresses key challenges in making evaluation more practical and scalable for real-world applications.

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Input: Task/Configuration              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Processing Layer                       │
│  - Analysis & Planning                  │
│  - Core Algorithm/Method                │
│  - Optimization                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Output: Result/Optimization            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Practical Guidance

### When to Use
- Use to systematically evaluate multi-turn editing agents and identify failure modes in object transformation tasks.
- When optimizing for agent
- In scenarios requiring editing

### When NOT to Use
- When simpler baselines suffice
- Without adequate computational resources
- For domains outside the paper's scope

### Common Pitfalls
- Not properly tuning hyperparameters
- Insufficient data for effective training
- Ignoring domain-specific constraints

### Hyperparameters

| Parameter | Default | Range | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-------|-------|
| `learning_rate` | 1e-4 | 1e-5 to 1e-2 | Adjust based on convergence |
| `batch_size` | 32 | 8 to 256 | Depends on GPU memory |
| `num_epochs` | 10 | 1 to 100 | More epochs = more training |
